welcomeLine: This is a test of the new four-section expand format. Click More on any story.
transitionLine: And here's one more for good measure

stories.headline: #QuitGPT: largest-ever OpenAI user boycott
stories.summary: triggered by OpenAI's $200M Pentagon military AI contract; 2.5M pledges, ChatGPT uninstalls up 295%, Claude hit #1 US App Store.
whatHappened: OpenAI signed a $200M contract to deploy AI inside Pentagon classified networks for autonomous weapons and surveillance on February 28.
ChatGPT uninstalls jumped 295% day-over-day the same day the contract leaked; 2.5M users signed pledges at quitgpt.org within a week.
Claude surged 51% in downloads and hit #1 on the US App Store. OpenAI robotics lead Caitlin Kalinowski resigned on principle.
whyItMatters: This is the first mass consumer boycott of an AI company triggered by a single contract. It reshuffled the competitive rankings in real time.
Anthropic had declined a similar Pentagon offer and was simultaneously blacklisted by the DoD as a supply chain risk, making the timing look deliberately predatory on OpenAI's part.
whatsNext: Sam Altman held an X AMA admitting the rollout 'looked opportunistic and sloppy' — watch whether OpenAI modifies the contract terms or doubles down.
If Claude holds the App Store top spot for more than a week, it marks a durable shift in brand perception, not just a protest bump.
yourMove: If you use ChatGPT for work, now's a good moment to run a side-by-side with Claude on your actual tasks. The gap has narrowed enough that the switch might stick.

stories.headline: Karpathy open-sources 'autoresearch': AI runs its own ML experiments
stories.summary: 630-line Python tool; 8.6M views, 8,000+ GitHub stars; one community member scaled to 333 experiments overnight.
whatHappened: Andrej Karpathy released a 630-line Python script that lets an AI agent autonomously run ML experiments on a single GPU, modifying training code and evaluating results in a loop.
Over 48 hours it ran roughly 100 experiments and cut nanotchat's benchmark training time by 11%, from 2.02 hours to 1.80 hours.
whyItMatters: Unsupervised AI-driven research is no longer a thought experiment. A single GPU owner can now run a self-improving experiment loop overnight with off-the-shelf hardware.
whatsNext: Karpathy is calling for a SETI@home-style distributed network of thousands of parallel agents running experiments around the clock. Community member Varun Mathur already scaled it to 333 parallel experiments overnight.
